nodes.name: Webhook — Receive Lead Request
nodes.notes: Entry point. POST body must include: keywords (array), region (string), sources (array), optional maxLeads (number). The webhook waits for the Respond node before replying.

nodes.name: Code — Parse Input & Build Query
nodes.notes: Validates input, applies defaults, and writes API keys into the pipeline context so downstream nodes can reference them via {{$json.GOOGLE_KEY}} etc.

nodes.name: HTTP — Google Places TextSearch
nodes.notes: Calls Google Maps Places TextSearch. Returns up to 20 raw business results matching the combined keyword + region query. Requires Places API enabled in GCP Console.

nodes.name: Code — Extract Place IDs as Items
nodes.notes: Splits the TextSearch array into N individual items. The next HTTP Request node for Place Details will automatically execute once per item (n8n's default looping behaviour).

nodes.name: HTTP — Google Place Details
nodes.notes: Fetches verified contact data for each place: phone number, website URL, full formatted address. Runs automatically N times (once per item from the Extract node). Fields billed separately — only request what you need.

nodes.name: Code — Collect Leads & Infer Emails
nodes.notes: Aggregates all per-place Detail responses into one item. Infers contact emails from website domains (info@domain.com pattern). Builds a single batch Gemini prompt for all leads — efficient API usage, one call instead of N.

nodes.name: HTTP — Gemini AI Batch Enrichment
nodes.notes: Single batch Gemini call — sends ALL leads in one prompt. Returns a JSON array with ai_summary, lead_score, and draft_email for each lead. Gemini 1.5 Flash is optimised for speed and low cost on Railway free tier.

nodes.name: Code — Build Final Response
nodes.notes: Merges Gemini AI data with verified Google Maps company data. Auto-assigns Hot/Warm/Cold priority from lead_score. Returns the final payload the frontend expects: { status, count, leads: [...] }.

nodes.name: Respond to Webhook
nodes.notes: Sends the final JSON response back to the caller (your SaaS frontend). CORS header included so browser fetch() requests succeed. Uses the output from the Build Final Response node.

main.node: Code — Extract Place IDs as Items
